Apple's Q1 2023 revenue declined over 5% year over year, the biggest decline since 2016 and first since 2019.
Apple's iPad revenue grew 29.66% to $9.4 billion in Q1 2023.
Apple's Other Products revenue was $13.48 billion in Q1 2023, down 8.3% year over year.
Apple's Mac revenue was $7.74 billion in Q1 2023, down almost 30% year over year.
Apple's board declared a cash dividend of $0.23 per share payable on February 16, 2023 to shareholders of record as of February 13, 2023.
Apple's Q1 2023 performance was affected by production and supply issues in China and a strong US dollar.
production and supply issues in China and strong US dollar
Apple announced financial results for fiscal 2023 Q1 ended December 31, 2022: quarterly revenue $117.2 billion, down 5% year over year; earnings per diluted share $1.88.
Apple generated $34 billion in operating cash flow and returned over $25 billion to shareholders during Q1 2023.
Apple cash flow and shareholder returns
Apple's iPhone revenue was $65.78 billion in Q1 2023, down 8.17% year over year.
Apple's Services business set an all-time revenue record of $20.8 billion in Q1 2023.
Apple's Services revenue grew 6.4% to $20.77 billion in Q1 2023.
Apple has more than 2 billion active devices as part of its growing installed base as of December 2022.
Apple has resolved many of its supply problems as of the earnings call date (February 2, 2023).
Apple declined to give guidance for the next quarter, as it has not done since the pandemic began in 2020.
